content.p: For purposes of Sections 2.212 through 2.215, the following definitions apply:

"Proclaimed emergency or disaster" means the president or governor has declared or proclaimed a state of emergency for an area that includes the geographic territory of the city or a local emergency has been proclaimed and is currently in effect, as provided in the Mountain View City Code.

"Public exigency or emergency" means a situation, related to a proclaimed emergency or disaster, which demands immediate aid or action and/or presents an immediate threat to public health, life, safety or improved property, as further defined in administrative regulations or policies.
"Purchase(s)"means the purchase(s), rental(s) or lease(s) of goods or services related to a proclaimed emergency or disaster.

"Simplified acquisition threshold" means the dollar amount below which a nonfederal entity may purchase property or services using small purchase methods, as set forth in Title 2 of the Code of Federal Regulations, Part 200, as it may be amended from time to time.
